intro: Odostemon hartwegii, also known as Hartweg's prickly ash, is a shrub-like plant native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, as well as its ability to aid in digestive issues and reduce pain. The bioactive constituents of Odostemon hartwegii include alkaloids, flavonoids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Hartweg's prickly ash include teas, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including arthritis and skin irritations.
